title: Skopeo signature generated for an image can be used while promoting the image from one environment to another?
body:
Can the same signature will be used for image verification while promoting the image from one environment to another where the target artifactory host is changing?

I have verified the signature file it has host present so when we use the same signature which we created at QA environment doesn't working when we verify at the staging environment as the artifactory host changed.

If its not possible then how can we achieve the image verification across environments where artifactory host is changing but the digest remains same.

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

Typically, I recommend designating an “authoritative identity” (frequently the public-internet image location, if any); and then, for local mirroring, using `registries.conf` to transparently redirect accesses; all deployment configuration would continue to use the authoritative identity, and does not need changing when the mirror setup changes.

Alternatively, `remapIdentity` can be used in `policy.json`.
… and, for “staging” signed builds on non-public registries before publishing them under the authoritative identity, see `--sign-identity`.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
